assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.label: Code-reading, debugging, and implementation challenge
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic represents a day-to-day coding skill. The topic quiz must primarily test code reading, debugging, output prediction, or implementation choice.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.

commonMistakes: Memorizing syntax without predicting the underlying value or execution order.
Mutating shared data when the task expects a new value or explicit ownership.
Treating browser APIs and language features as the same layer.
Choosing an abstraction from habit rather than required operations.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Compare primitives versus objects using a new example and justify the result.
Explain stack and heap mental model using a new example and justify the result.
Explain references using a new example and justify the result.
Explain garbage collection using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Primitives versus objects** → **Stack and heap mental model** → **References** → **Garbage collection**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Memory Management and References rule and the observable result.
choices.label: Object variables hold references. Equality checks identity rather than structural similarity, and shallow copies may still share nested objects.

choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Memory Management and References rule and the observable result.
choices.label: Garbage collection reclaims values that are no longer reachable. Event listeners, timers, caches, and closures can accidentally retain references and extend lifetimes.

choices.feedback: Assigning a primitive copies its value. Assigning an object copies a reference to the same object, so mutations are visible through every reference.
choices.label: Assigning a primitive copies its value. Assigning an object copies a reference to the same object, so mutations are visible through every reference.

choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Memory Management and References rule and the observable result.

choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Memory Management and References rule and the observable result.
choices.label: Freeze only the outer copy after mutating it.

choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Memory Management and References rule and the observable result.
choices.label: Assign `copy = settings` explicitly.

choices.feedback: Copy the nested level as well—`{ ...settings, theme: { ...settings.theme, name: 'dark' } }`—or use an appropriate deep-clone strategy.
choices.label: Copy the nested level as well—`{ ...settings, theme: { ...settings.theme, name: 'dark' } }`—or use an appropriate deep-clone strategy.

choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Memory Management and References rule and the observable result.
choices.label: Delete the nested object before reading it.

questions.explanation: Copy the nested level as well—`{ ...settings, theme: { ...settings.theme, name: 'dark' } }`—or use an appropriate deep-clone strategy.

summary: Memory Management and References connects Primitives versus objects, Stack and heap mental model, References.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: Memory Management and References

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: This topic turns memory management and references from isolated terminology into a practical tool for reading, writing, and reviewing code. It belongs to the **How JavaScript Works** module.
